A majority of news consumers bounce between different sources so they can "form their own view" - on all sides of the aisle - but how many news outlets embrace that kind of behavior? Or encourage it? Or augment it and make it easier?

My lesson from The Markup: the future of journalism is not taking large sums of money/power and putting it in the control of a few players in traditionally hierarchical organizations (it probably looks like literally anything but that!)
